sn44.7:1.1.pli: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahāmoggallānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn44.7:1.1.eng: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, and exchanged greetings with him.

sn44.7:1.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
sn44.7:1.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to Mahāmoggallāna:

sn44.7:2.1.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, sassato loko”ti?
sn44.7:2.1.eng: “Worthy Moggallāna, is this right: ‘the cosmos is eternal’?”

sn44.7:2.2.eng: “Vaccha, this has not been declared by the Buddha.”

sn44.7:2.4.eng: “Then is this right: ‘the cosmos is not eternal’ …

sn44.7:2.7.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, antavā loko”ti?

sn44.7:2.13.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran”ti?
sn44.7:2.13.eng: ‘the soul and the body are identical’ …

sn44.7:2.16.pli: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran”ti?
sn44.7:2.16.eng: ‘the soul is one thing, the body another’ …

sn44.7:2.19.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
sn44.7:2.19.eng: ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …

sn44.7:2.22.pli: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
sn44.7:2.22.eng: ‘a realized one no longer exists after death’ …

sn44.7:2.25.pli: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
sn44.7:2.25.eng: ‘a realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death’ …

sn44.7:2.27.pli: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.

sn44.7:2.28.pli: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
sn44.7:2.28.eng: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”

sn44.7:2.29.eng: “This too has not been declared by the Buddha.”

sn44.7:2.30.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.

sn44.7:3.1.pli: “Ko nu kho, bho moggallāna, hetu ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
sn44.7:3.1.eng: “What’s the cause, worthy Moggallāna, what’s the reason why, when the wanderers of other religions are asked these questions, they declare one of these to be true?

sn44.7:3.2.pli: sassato lokoti vā, asassato lokoti vā, antavā lokoti vā, anantavā lokoti vā, taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti vā, aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīranti vā, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā?

sn44.7:3.3.pli: Ko pana, bho moggallāna, hetu ko paccayo, yena samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
sn44.7:3.3.eng: And what’s the reason why, when the ascetic Gotama is asked these questions, he does not declare one of these to be true?”

sn44.7:3.4.pli: sassato lokotipi, asassato lokotipi, antavā lokotipi, anantavā lokotipi, taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīrantipi, aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīrantipi, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipi, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipi, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipi, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipī”ti?

sn44.7:4.1.pli: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā cakkhuṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…
sn44.7:4.1.eng: “Vaccha, the wanderers of other religions regard the eye like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’ They regard the ear … nose …

sn44.7:4.2.pli: jivhaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…

sn44.7:4.3.pli: manaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti.
sn44.7:4.3.eng: mind like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’

sn44.7:4.4.pli: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
sn44.7:4.4.eng: That’s why, when asked, they declare one of those answers to be true.

sn44.7:4.6.pli: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā.

sn44.7:4.7.pli: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho cakkhuṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…
sn44.7:4.7.eng: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha regards the eye like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’ He regards the ear … nose …

sn44.7:4.8.pli: jivhaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…

sn44.7:4.9.pli: manaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati.
sn44.7:4.9.eng: mind like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’

sn44.7:4.10.pli: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
sn44.7:4.10.eng: That’s why, when asked, he does not declare one of those answers to be true.”

sn44.7:4.12.pli: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipī”ti.

sn44.7:5.1.pli: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn44.7:5.1.eng: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta got up from his seat and went to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.

sn44.7:5.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn44.7:5.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side. He asked the Buddha the same questions, and received the same answers.

sn44.7:5.3.pli: “kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, sassato loko”ti?

sn44.7:5.5.pli: “Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?

sn44.7:5.7.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.

sn44.7:6.1.pli: “Ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.7:6.3.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā?

sn44.7:6.4.pli: Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo, yena bhoto gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.7:6.6.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti?

sn44.7:7.1.pli: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā cakkhuṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…

sn44.7:7.2.pli: jivhaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…

sn44.7:7.3.pli: manaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti.

sn44.7:7.4.pli: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.7:7.6.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.

sn44.7:7.7.pli: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho cakkhuṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…

sn44.7:7.8.pli: jivhaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…

sn44.7:7.9.pli: manaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati.

sn44.7:7.10.pli: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.7:7.11.pli: ‘sassato loko’tipi, ‘asassato loko’tipi, ‘antavā loko’tipi, ‘anantavā loko’tipi, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti.

sn44.7:8.1.eng: He said, “It’s incredible, worthy Gotama, it’s amazing!

sn44.7:8.2.pli: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati samessati na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmiṁ.
sn44.7:8.2.eng: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!

sn44.7:8.3.pli: Idānāhaṁ, bho gotama, samaṇaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ apucchiṁ.
sn44.7:8.3.eng: Just now I went to the ascetic Mahāmoggallāna and asked him about this matter.

sn44.7:8.4.pli: Samaṇopi me moggallāno etehi padehi etehi byañjanehi tamatthaṁ byākāsi, seyyathāpi bhavaṁ gotamo.
sn44.7:8.4.eng: And he explained it to me with these words and phrases, just like the worthy Gotama.

sn44.7:8.5.pli: Acchariyaṁ, bho gotama, abbhutaṁ, bho gotama.
sn44.7:8.5.eng: It’s incredible, worthy Gotama, it’s amazing!

sn44.7:8.6.pli: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati samessati na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmin”ti.
sn44.7:8.6.eng: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!”

sn44.8:1.1.pli: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn44.8:1.1.eng: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.

sn44.8:1.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn44.8:1.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:

sn44.8:1.3.pli: “kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, sassato loko”ti?
sn44.8:1.3.eng: “Worthy Gotama, is this right: ‘the cosmos is eternal’?”

sn44.8:1.4.eng: “This has not been declared by me, Vaccha.” …

sn44.8:1.5.pli: “Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
sn44.8:1.5.eng: “Then is this right: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”

sn44.8:1.6.eng: “This too has not been declared by me.”

sn44.8:1.7.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.

sn44.8:2.1.pli: “Ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
sn44.8:2.1.eng: “What’s the cause, worthy Gotama, what’s the reason why, when the wanderers of other religions are asked these questions, they declare one of these to be true?

sn44.8:2.3.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā?

sn44.8:2.4.pli: Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo, yena bhoto gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
sn44.8:2.4.eng: And what’s the reason why, when the worthy Gotama is asked these questions, he does not declare one of these to be true?”

sn44.8:2.6.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti?

sn44.8:3.1.pli: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā rūpaṁ attato samanupassanti, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
sn44.8:3.1.eng: “Vaccha, the wanderers of other religions regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.

sn44.8:3.5.pli: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassanti, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
sn44.8:3.5.eng: consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.

sn44.8:3.6.pli: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
sn44.8:3.6.eng: That’s why, when asked, they declare one of those answers to be true.

sn44.8:3.8.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.

sn44.8:3.9.pli: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho na rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
sn44.8:3.9.eng: The Realized One doesn’t regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.

sn44.8:3.13.pli: na viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, na viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā viññāṇaṁ, na viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
sn44.8:3.13.eng: consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.

sn44.8:3.14.pli: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
sn44.8:3.14.eng: That’s why, when asked, he does not declare one of those answers to be true.”

sn44.8:3.16.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti.

sn44.8:4.1.pli: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako uṭṭhāyāsanā yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahāmoggallānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn44.8:4.1.eng: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta got up from his seat and went to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, and exchanged greetings with him.

sn44.8:4.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
sn44.8:4.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side. He asked Mahāmoggallāna the same questions, and received the same answers.

sn44.8:4.3.pli: “kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, sassato loko”ti?

sn44.8:4.5.pli: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?

sn44.8:4.7.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.

sn44.8:5.1.pli: “Ko nu kho, bho moggallāna, hetu, ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.8:5.3.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā?

sn44.8:5.4.pli: Ko pana, bho moggallāna, hetu, ko paccayo yena samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.8:5.6.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti?

sn44.8:6.1.pli: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā rūpaṁ attato samanupassanti, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.

sn44.8:6.5.pli: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassanti, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.

sn44.8:6.6.pli: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.8:6.8.pli: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.

sn44.8:6.9.pli: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho na rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.

sn44.8:6.13.pli: na viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, na viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā viññāṇaṁ, na viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.

sn44.8:6.14.pli: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:

sn44.8:6.15.pli: ‘sassato loko’tipi, ‘asassato loko’tipi, ‘antavā loko’tipi, ‘anantavā loko’tipi, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti.

sn44.8:7.1.eng: He said, “It’s incredible, worthy Moggallāna, it’s amazing.

sn44.8:7.2.pli: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati, samessati, na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmiṁ.
sn44.8:7.2.eng: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!

sn44.8:7.3.pli: Idānāhaṁ, bho moggallāna, samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ apucchiṁ.
sn44.8:7.3.eng: Just now I went to the ascetic Gotama and asked him about this matter.

sn44.8:7.4.pli: Samaṇopi me gotamo etehi padehi etehi byañjanehi etamatthaṁ byākāsi, seyyathāpi bhavaṁ moggallāno.
sn44.8:7.4.eng: And he explained it to me with these words and phrases, just like the worthy Moggallāna.

sn44.8:7.5.pli: Acchariyaṁ, bho moggallāna, abbhutaṁ, bho moggallāna.
sn44.8:7.5.eng: It’s incredible, worthy Moggallāna, it’s amazing!

sn44.8:7.6.pli: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati samessati na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmin”ti.
sn44.8:7.6.eng: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!”

sn44.9:1.1.pli: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn44.9:1.1.eng: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.

sn44.9:1.2.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn44.9:1.2.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:

sn44.9:2.1.pli: “Purimāni, bho gotama, divasāni purimatarāni sambahulānaṁ nānātitthiyānaṁ samaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ kutūhalasālāyaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
sn44.9:2.1.eng: “Worthy Gotama, a few days ago several ascetics, brahmins, and wanderers who follow various other religions were sitting together in the debating hall, and this discussion came up among them:

sn44.9:2.2.pli: ‘ayaṁ kho pūraṇo kassapo saṅghī ceva gaṇī ca gaṇācariyo ca ñāto yasassī titthakaro sādhusammato bahujanassa.
sn44.9:2.2.eng: ‘This Pūraṇa Kassapa leads an order and a community, and tutors a community. He’s a well-known and famous religious founder, deemed holy by many people.

sn44.9:2.3.pli: Sopi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
sn44.9:2.3.eng: When a disciple passes away, he declares that

sn44.9:2.4.eng: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.

sn44.9:2.5.pli: Yopissa sāvako uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto tampi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
sn44.9:2.5.eng: And as for a disciple who is a supreme person, highest of people, having reached the highest point, when they pass away he also declares that

sn44.9:2.6.eng: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.

sn44.9:3.2.eng: the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan …

sn44.9:3.5.pli: ayampi kho ajito kesakambalo saṅghī ceva gaṇī ca gaṇācariyo ca ñāto yasassī titthakaro sādhusammato bahujanassa.
sn44.9:3.5.eng: Ajita of the hair blanket leads an order and a community, and tutors a community. He’s a well-known and famous religious founder, deemed holy by many people.

sn44.9:3.6.pli: Sopi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
sn44.9:3.6.eng: When a disciple passes away, he declares that

sn44.9:3.7.eng: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.

sn44.9:3.8.pli: Yopissa sāvako uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto tampi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
sn44.9:3.8.eng: And as for a disciple who is a supreme person, highest of people, having reached the highest point, when they pass away he also declares that

sn44.9:3.9.eng: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.

sn44.9:4.1.pli: Ayampi kho samaṇo gotamo saṅghī ceva gaṇī ca gaṇācariyo ca ñāto yasassī titthakaro sādhusammato bahujanassa.
sn44.9:4.1.eng: This ascetic Gotama leads an order and a community, and tutors a community. He’s a well-known and famous religious founder, deemed holy by many people.

sn44.9:4.2.pli: Sopi sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu byākaroti:
sn44.9:4.2.eng: When a disciple passes away, he declares that

sn44.9:4.3.eng: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.

sn44.9:4.4.pli: Yopissa sāvako uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto tañca sāvakaṁ abbhatītaṁ kālaṅkataṁ upapattīsu na byākaroti:
sn44.9:4.4.eng: But as for a disciple who is a supreme person, highest of people, having reached the highest point, when they pass away he doesn’t declare that

sn44.9:4.5.eng: this one is reborn here, while that one is reborn there.

sn44.9:4.6.pli: Api ca kho naṁ evaṁ byākaroti:
sn44.9:4.6.eng: Rather, he declares that they have

sn44.9:4.7.pli: ‘acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā’ti.
sn44.9:4.7.eng: cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit have made an end of suffering.’

sn44.9:4.8.pli: Tassa mayhaṁ, bho gotama, ahu deva kaṅkhā, ahu vicikicchā:
sn44.9:4.8.eng: I had doubt and uncertainty about that:

sn44.9:4.9.eng: ‘How on earth can I understand the ascetic Gotama’s teaching?’”

sn44.9:5.1.eng: “Vaccha, no wonder you’re doubting and uncertain.

sn44.9:5.2.pli: Kaṅkhanīye ca pana te ṭhāne vicikicchā uppannā.
sn44.9:5.2.eng: Doubt has come up in you about an uncertain matter.

sn44.9:5.3.pli: Saupādānassa khvāhaṁ, vaccha, upapattiṁ paññāpemi no anupādānassa.
sn44.9:5.3.eng: I describe rebirth for someone who has fuel for grasping, not for someone who does not.

sn44.9:5.4.pli: Seyyathāpi, vaccha, aggi saupādāno jalati, no anupādāno;
sn44.9:5.4.eng: It’s like a fire which only burns with fuel, not without fuel.

sn44.9:5.5.pli: evameva khvāhaṁ, vaccha, saupādānassa upapattiṁ paññāpemi, no anupādānassā”ti.
sn44.9:5.5.eng: In the same way I describe rebirth for someone who grasps fuel, not for someone who doesn’t grasp fuel.”

sn44.9:6.1.pli: “Yasmiṁ, bho gotama, samaye acci vātena khittā dūrampi gacchati, imassa pana bhavaṁ gotamo kiṁ upādānasmiṁ paññāpetī”ti?
sn44.9:6.1.eng: “But when a flame is blown away by the wind, what does the worthy Gotama say is its fuel then?”

sn44.9:6.2.pli: “Yasmiṁ kho, vaccha, samaye acci vātena khittā dūrampi gacchati, tamahaṁ vātūpādānaṁ paññāpemi.
sn44.9:6.2.eng: “At such a time, I say that it’s fueled by the wind.

sn44.9:6.3.pli: Vāto hissa, vaccha, tasmiṁ samaye upādānaṁ hotī”ti.
sn44.9:6.3.eng: For the wind is its fuel then.”

sn44.9:6.4.pli: “Yasmiñca pana, bho gotama, samaye imañca kāyaṁ nikkhipati, satto ca aññataraṁ kāyaṁ anupapanno hoti, imassa pana bhavaṁ gotamo kiṁ upādānasmiṁ paññāpetī”ti?
sn44.9:6.4.eng: “But when someone who is attached has laid down this body and has not been reborn in one of the realms, what does the worthy Gotama say is their fuel then?”

sn44.9:6.5.pli: “Yasmiṁ kho, vaccha, samaye imañca kāyaṁ nikkhipati, satto ca aññataraṁ kāyaṁ anupapanno hoti, tamahaṁ taṇhūpādānaṁ vadāmi.
sn44.9:6.5.eng: “When someone who is attached has laid down this body, Vaccha, and has not been reborn in one of the realms, I say they’re fueled by craving.

sn44.9:6.6.pli: Taṇhā hissa, vaccha, tasmiṁ samaye upādānaṁ hotī”ti.
sn44.9:6.6.eng: For craving is their fuel then.”
